MASTERING CEMETERY MANAGEMENT: Essential Strategies for Modern Graveyard Care​
In today’s world, effective cemetery management goes far beyond the simple maintenance of gravestones and landscapes; it's about honoring the lives of those who have passed while providing a serene environment for visitors. As communities evolve and the needs of families change, modern graveyard care requires innovative strategies that blend respect, technology, and sustainable practices. From utilizing cutting-edge tools for inventory management to enhancing visitor engagement through thoughtful design, cemetery administrators now face the challenge—and opportunity—of modernizing their approach.

How do you Digitize Cemetery Records?
In an earlier blog, we discussed why your cemetery should consider transitioning from paper records to digital records. Digitizing cemetery records is a valuable step for efficiency and accessibility. Here's a general step-by-step process to help you with the transition:

​Assessment: Evaluate the current record system and identify what needs to be digitized. This may include burial plots, interment records, maps, and more.  It's not uncommon to have interment cards and sales contracts in abundance.  Knowing how many records you need to digitize is the first step towards assembling a transition plan.

Trends in Green Burial
As awareness grows about sustainable practices many people are adopting greener lifestyles. In more and more cases, they are choosing to forgo a traditional burial because it is very taxing to the earth and its resources.

​Green burials are eco-friendly ways to minimize your carbon footprint one last time. While most natural burials involve placing unembalmed bodies into the ground where they can merge with the earth, there are in fact several types of green burial methods available.

It is important to become knowledgeable about the various kinds of green burials, so you can decide which one is best for you.

Breaking Down the Myths and Facts About Grief
As the holidays draw near, it is important to remember that this is not a time of joy for everyone. Many people are missing a loved one who has passed away while others are feeling nostalgic for simpler times. Regardless of why a person is feeling sorrowful, it is necessary to understand what grief is and how to comfort those who are experiencing this strong emotion.  
​
Grief Defined   
According to the Mayo Clinic, grief is a natural reaction to loss. It is a strong, sometimes overwhelming emotion that leaves people feeling numb and removed from daily life. They are often unable to carry on with regular duties.

CELEBRATION OF LIFE SOFTWARE & APP
FOR PLOT OWNERS OR NEXT OF KIN
​Whether you're pre-need planning or at-need, you or your loved one can leave an everlasting digital footprint.
FOR FAMILY & FRIENDS OF THE DECEASED
Coming 2023, download the EverArk celebration of life app and share comments, photos, tributes, and more to commemorate your loved one.
We have a feature rich cemetery management software.  And, if you see something you need that we don't have, we will build it.
